rulings.Case Title: ELVIA E PINEDA VS. BANK OF AMERICA N.A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 8, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANTs BANK OF AMERICA N.A and RECONTRUST COMPANY N.A.'s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to address all issues raised by demurrer in good faith. Plaintiff shall have 30 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. The Court notes that there may be a necessary party in this action, trustee of the special needs trust, title owner of the property prior to foreclosure. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: LAI FONG LEONG ET AL VS. CHAN 1985 TRUST ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 8, 2019 line 8. DEFENDANTs LAWRENCE CHAN and PAULINE CHAN's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED prejudice for Plaintiff to bring a proper motion for leave to amend (or any other appropriate relief). The Court notes that the trial in this action is set for April 22, 2019 on preference, the last day being April 26, 2019. Plaintiff is to address the approaching trial date before or simultaneously with any motion for relief re: leave to amend. In her Second Amended Complaint and in opposition to the demurer Plaintiff fails to (1) address any authorities cited by Defendant; (2) explain why Plaintiff failed to comply with Court's March 7, 2019 order giving Plaintiff limited leave to amend; (3) explain why Plaintiff filed an amended complaint in its present form without leave of Court; (4) explain why Plaintiff delayed seeking leave to amend for a month (until April 3, 2019) while admitting that the facts giving rise to new allegations were discovered on February 28, 2019 and in light of April 22, 2019 trial; (5) explain why Plaintiff failed to address the matter when Plaintiff was alerted on March 18, 2019 that the Second Amended Complaint was improperly filed; (6) explain why Plaintiff failed to participate in meet and confer in good faith, i.e. immediately seek leave to amend and delayed such request for 2 weeks; (7) explain why Plaintiff disregarded procedural requirements for properly filing second amended complaint; (8) explain why the facts giving rise to the amendment have not been previously discovered. Additionally, in light of procedural posture of this case Plaintiff's failure to timely seek leave to amend results in Defendant not being able to have sufficient time to oppose the leave. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: LAI FONG LEONG ET AL VS. CHAN 1985 TRUST ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 8, 2019 line 8. DEFENDANTs LAWRENCE CHAN, CHAN 1985 TRUST, and PAULINE CHAN's MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT GRANTED. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BRANISLAV RADIKOVIC VS. YING FAI CHAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 8, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT YING CHAN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to causes of action 2 to 6 based on waiver are overruled. Waiver excluded claims under SFRO 37.9(a)(8). Parties' Requests for Judicial Notice are granted. Demurrer to the cause of action for injunction is sustained with leave to amend for it to be re-asserted as a remedy. Demurrer to the first cause of action is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts underlying his "information and belief" formed on October 2, 2018 that Defendant did not live at the subject property. Demurer to the causes of action for breach of covenant of quiet enjoyment is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting his assertion that he a third party beneficiary of the master lease, i.e. that there was an express sublease provision in the Master Lease. Marchese v. Standard Realty & Develop. Co. (1977) 74 Cal.App.3d 142; Real Property Services v. City of Pasadena (1994) 25 Cal.App.4th 375. Demurrer to cause of action for violation of SFRO 37.9B is overruled. This cause of action is sufficiently alleged and Plaintiff has standing as a subtenant (SFRO 37.9B, UD Complaint, Rent Board Decision). Demurrer to negligence cause of action is OVERRULED. Negligence is sufficiently alleged. NOTE: Plaintiff's fifth cause of action is titled "Negligence (including Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress)." In opposition Plaintiff concedes that Negligence Infliction of Emotional Distress is not alleged. Reference to Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress shall be removed from the amended complaint. = (501/CFH)
